title: Protocol: pharmaceutical trial investment in Africa among multinational companies
primary_estimand: Africa extraction ratio (Africa trials / global trials) per company
body: This cross-sectional registry study will examine how much of major multinational pharmaceutical trial activity includes African clinical sites. We will query ClinicalTrials.gov API v2 for all interventional studies sponsored by ten large pharmaceutical companies worldwide and for the subset listing at least one African location across 20 specified countries. The primary outcome is the Africa extraction ratio, calculated as African trials divided by global trials for each company, with a ratio below the population-proportional benchmark indicating under-investment. Secondary analyses will describe therapeutic area distribution, phase breakdown, country concentration within Africa, enrollment figures, and the balance between Africa-focused studies addressing local disease burden and multinational programmes enrolling African participants for regulatory convenience. Classification rules for extraction, partnership, and mixed engagement patterns will be defined before analysis and archived with all code. All data are public registry records and no participant-level information will be accessed. Activity registered only on national or WHO regional platforms will not be captured.
ai_transparency: LLM assistance was used for drafting and language editing. The author reviewed and edited the manuscript and takes responsibility for the final content.

checks.name: methods described
checks.detail: Protocol describes API query strategy, classification scheme, and outcomes.

checks.name: outcomes specified
checks.detail: Primary and secondary outcomes defined in sentence 6.
